title: setparam makes hierarchy fail
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.33+103 (git sha1 aeb742b8b, gcc 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

`x.v`:
```verilog
module topmodule #(parameter top_param = 1) ();

submodule #(.sub_param(top_param)) x();

endmodule
module submodule #(parameter sub_param = 1) ();
endmodule 
```

`synth.tcl`:
```tcl
yosys read_verilog -I. x.v

# This works
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule

# Also works
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule -chparam top_param 1

# This fails
yosys setparam -set top_param 1 topmodule
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule
```

Run `yosys -c synth.tcl`

### Expected Behavior

All three `hierarchy` commands work.

### Actual Behavior

Running `hierarchy` after `setparam` fails.

NB: the same behavior happens with v0.33 (Yosys 0.33 (git sha1 2584903a0, gcc 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -Os))
code_blocks:
yosys read_verilog -I. x.v

# This works
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule

# Also works
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule -chparam top_param 1

# This fails
yosys setparam -set top_param 1 topmodule
yosys hierarchy -check -top topmodule
